# Hi, I'm Xuanyou Liu (Zed)

```python
class Zed:
    def __init__(self):
        self.role = "Ph.D. Student in Computer Science @ Northwestern University"
        self.group = "MU Collective"
        self.advisor = "Jessica Hullman"
        self.focus = [
            "Human-AI Interaction",
            "Wearable Computing",
            "Haptics",
        ]
        self.current_projects = [
            "Decision making for multi-agent AI systems",
            "EIT-based hand pose estimation",
            "Wearable haptic interfaces",
        ]
```

## About

I am a Ph.D. student in Computer Science at Northwestern University, advised by [Jessica Hullman](https://users.eecs.northwestern.edu/~jhullman/) and affiliated with [MU Collective](https://mucollective.northwestern.edu/). My research sits at the intersection of human-AI interaction, wearable computing, and haptic interfaces.

I study how intelligent systems and novel sensing technologies can augment human perception and support more effective decision-making. My work spans interactive systems, uncertainty-aware decision support, wearable sensing, electrotactile interfaces, and embedded hardware.

Previously, I worked with [Pedro Lopes](https://lab.plopes.org/) at the University of Chicago's HC-Integration Lab. I received my M.S.E. in Robotics from the University of Pennsylvania and my B.E. in Industrial Design from Xi'an Jiaotong University.

## Current Interests

- Human-AI interaction and decision-making under uncertainty
- Wearable computing and novel sensing systems
- Haptic interfaces and electrotactile stimulation
- EIT-based hand pose estimation and input sensing

## Selected Work

| Project | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| **[MARIO](https://xuanyouliu.com)** | Motion-augmented multi-sensor inertial odometry for lightweight, camera-free human tracking. Findings of CVPR 2026 |
| **[Beyond Prompting](https://github.com/XuanyouLiu/silicon-sampling-mcp)** | A cognitively grounded framework and MCP implementation for silicon survey samples. Under review at IC2S2 2026 |
| **[Seeing with the Hands](https://doi.org/10.1145/3706598.3713419)** | A back-of-the-hand electrotactile display for sensory substitution that supports manual interactions. CHI 2025 |
| **[TacTex](https://doi.org/10.1145/3613904.3642873)** | A textile interface with seamlessly integrated electrodes for high-resolution electrotactile stimulation. CHI 2024 |
| **EITWatch** | Watch-back electrical impedance sensing for smartwatch gesture recognition. Under review at UIST 2026 |

## Background

- Ph.D. in Computer Science, Northwestern University
- M.S.E. in Robotics, University of Pennsylvania
- B.E. in Industrial Design, Xi'an Jiaotong University
